edges.description: Loss of osteonectin collagen binding impairs collagen fibril maturation and compromises osteoblast formation, maturation, and survival, degrading bone matrix quality.
edges.source: SPARC Loss-of-Function Disrupts Osteonectin Collagen Binding in the Bone ECM
edges.target: Impaired Bone Matrix Maturation and Osteoblast Function

edges.description: Impaired collagen matrix maturation and osteoblast function yield a defective bone extracellular matrix with reduced bone mass and mechanical strength, producing severe skeletal fragility.
edges.source: Impaired Bone Matrix Maturation and Osteoblast Function
edges.target: Defective Bone Matrix, Reduced Bone Mineral Density, and Skeletal Fragility

nodes.description: The disturbed osteonectin-dependent collagen matrix and impaired osteoblast function produce a defective bone extracellular matrix with abnormal mineralization. Although trabecular bone material can be hypermineralized at the material level, the overall result is reduced bone mass and mechanical strength with severe bone fragility. Osteonectin-null mice develop profound osteopenia from decreased bone formation, recapitulating the reduced bone mass of OI type XVII. Clinically this manifests as recurrent fractures, low bone mineral density, growth deficiency, vertebral compression, and bone deformity.
nodes.id: Defective Bone Matrix, Reduced Bone Mineral Density, and Skeletal Fragility

evidence.reference_title: Osteopenia and decreased bone formation in osteonectin-deficient mice.
evidence.snippet: We found that osteonectin-null mice have decreased bone formation and decreased osteoblast and osteoclast surface and number, leading to decreased bone remodeling with a negative bone balance and causing profound osteopenia.

evidence.reference_title: Recessive osteogenesis imperfecta caused by missense mutations in SPARC.
evidence.snippet: these observations show that homozygous mutations in SPARC can give rise to severe bone fragility in humans.

nodes.description: Osteonectin supports bone remodeling and the maintenance of bone mass; its loss compromises osteoblast formation, maturation, and survival and impairs collagen fibril organization within the bone extracellular matrix. In OI type XVII patient fibroblasts, SPARC secretion is reduced and collagen type I secretion is mildly delayed with some overmodification, reflecting disturbed matrix maturation. Osteonectin-null mice show decreased bone formation and decreased osteoblast number and surface, the cellular basis of the matrix defect.
nodes.id: Impaired Bone Matrix Maturation and Osteoblast Function

evidence.reference_title: Osteonectin-null mutation compromises osteoblast formation, maturation, and survival.
evidence.snippet: These data indicate that osteonectin is important for osteoblast formation, maturation, and survival.

evidence.reference_title: Osteonectin-null mutation compromises osteoblast formation, maturation, and survival.
evidence.snippet: Mutant cells exhibited decreased formation of mineralized nodules, as well as diminished expression of osteocalcin mRNA and response to PTH.

evidence.reference_title: Recessive osteogenesis imperfecta caused by missense mutations in SPARC.
evidence.snippet: Pulse-chase experiments showed that collagen type I secretion was mildly delayed in skin fibroblasts from both individuals.

nodes.description: SPARC encodes osteonectin, a secreted collagen-binding matricellular glycoprotein and one of the most abundant noncollagenous proteins in bone. Osteonectin binds collagen type I and other extracellular-matrix proteins and regulates collagen fibril assembly and matrix maturation. The defining OI type XVII missense variants (p.Arg166His, p.Glu263Lys) substitute residues that form an intramolecular salt bridge essential for SPARC binding to collagen type I, so the proximal lesion is loss of the collagen-binding matricellular function of secreted osteonectin in the bone ECM — distinct from the intracellular collagen-folding/modification recessive OI types.
nodes.id: SPARC Loss-of-Function Disrupts Osteonectin Collagen Binding in the Bone ECM

evidence.reference_title: Recessive osteogenesis imperfecta caused by missense mutations in SPARC.
evidence.snippet: Secreted protein, acidic, cysteine-rich (SPARC) is a glycoprotein that binds to collagen type I and other proteins in the extracellular matrix.

evidence.reference_title: Recessive osteogenesis imperfecta caused by missense mutations in SPARC.
evidence.snippet: the residues substituted by these mutations form an intramolecular salt bridge in SPARC and are essential for the binding of SPARC to collagen type I.
